_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass (SWORD VARIANT) — take the L2 'Outfitted Levy' composited keeper and produce the L3 'Veteran Levy' variant (Tier 1 top).
_meta.input_image_anchor: UnitProgression/Infantry/Refs/L1_Sword_Base.png (1024x1024 native — scale and framing reference only, content not applied)
history: v1 (2026-05-07): generated by build_sword_prompts.py from `infantry_edit_L2_to_L3.json` (mace variant). Bulk language substitutions: mace→sword, wooden haft→leather grip, iron spikes→cross-guard, mace head→sword blade, etc. Sword-specific weapon beats applied at L3, L4, L5, L6, L7, L10 (see WEAPON_BEAT_OVERRIDES in build_sword_prompts.py for the per-level deltas).
_meta.input_strategy: Within-tier chained (Rule 2): two inputs = L2 composite + L1_Sword_Base scale anchor.

prompt: CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION (READ FIRST) — The first input image is the canonical content reference. It already shows the L2 'Outfitted Levy' kit on top of the L1 base. You MUST preserve every L2-introduced element exactly as shown in the first input image: (a) QUILTED BROWN LEATHER SHORT SLEEVES on both upper arms — matte warm-brown leather with diagonal cross-stitching pattern, covering shoulder to mid-bicep, with a roughly 1-inch gap of bare warm peachy-tan skin visible between the lower edge of each sleeve and the upper edge of each leather forearm bracer; (b) WIDE WARM-BROWN LEATHER WAIST BELT with a square dark-iron buckle centered at the front and visible edge stitching — the belt is NOT a rope cord, NOT a thin strap; (c) SMALL BROWN LEATHER HIP POUCH with flap closure and small brass stud, hanging from the leather belt on the anatomical-RIGHT hip (viewer-LEFT side) — exactly ONE pouch on that side; (d) EIGHT DARK-IRON ROUND-HEAD RIVETS evenly spaced as a ring around the wooden rim of the round shield. DO NOT revert any of these L2 features back to the L1 state. DO NOT remove the leather sleeves and re-expose bare biceps. DO NOT replace the leather belt with a rope cord. DO NOT remove the hip pouch. DO NOT remove the rim rivets. The second input image is provided ONLY as a scale and framing reference — match its character-to-canvas proportions exactly; do NOT apply any of its content (the second input image is the bare L1 baseline without the L2 kit; only use it for scale, framing, and pose reference). EDIT GOAL — Apply FOUR visible additive upgrades to the infantryman shown in the first input image, all building on the existing L2 kit. The four upgrades together represent a 'Veteran Levy' rank step — the conscript footman has earned his veteran-levy kit. (1) SINGLE STEEL PAULDRON CAP ON THE ANATOMICAL-RIGHT SHOULDER — add a small rounded steel cap covering only the anatomical-RIGHT shoulder (the sword-arm side, viewer-LEFT side of frame). The pauldron is approximately 4 inches in diameter, cool silver-gray polished steel, slight domed curve, with three visible dark-iron rivets along its lower seam where it attaches to a thin warm-brown leather strap that runs across the chest underneath the existing livery tunic (the leather strap is mostly hidden under the tunic but visible briefly at each shoulder seam). The pauldron sits ON TOP OF the existing quilted leather short sleeve, partially covering the shoulder portion of that sleeve. The anatomical-LEFT shoulder (shield-arm side, viewer-RIGHT) remains UNCOVERED — no pauldron there at this level. (2) BRASS QUILLON CAPS AT THE CROSS-GUARD TIPS — add small brass bulb caps at each end of the existing simple steel cross-guard. Each brass cap is a rounded bulb approximately ¾ inch in diameter, matte warm brass (slightly duller than gold — clearly brass, not yellow paint, not gold) with a soft top-down highlight, sitting flush at the outer tip of each cross-guard arm. The cross-guard body itself remains cool silver-gray polished steel; only the two outer tips become brass caps.  (3) FOUR IRON STRAP-BANDS RADIATING FROM THE SHIELD BOSS — add four short flat iron strap-bands on the wooden face of the round shield, each band radiating outward from the central steel boss to the rim like the four arms of a cross (one band points up, one down, one to the viewer-left, one to the viewer-right). Each band is approximately 1 inch wide, dark cool-gray iron, riveted at three points along its length, ends just shy of the existing iron rim rivet ring. The wooden plank face of the shield remains visible BETWEEN the four cross arms; the L2 ring of eight rim rivets remains; the central steel boss remains. (4) BROWN LEATHER KNIFE SHEATH ON THE ANATOMICAL-LEFT HIP — add a small simple brown leather knife sheath hanging from the leather waist belt on his anatomical-LEFT hip (the viewer-RIGHT side of the belt line, OPPOSITE the existing L2 hip pouch). The sheath is approximately 5 inches tall and 1.5 inches wide, matte warm-brown leather with a single horizontal binding strap. A small bone-handle knife is visible at the top of the sheath — only the cream-bone handle is visible (the blade is fully sheathed). The sheath hangs vertically against the hip. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE FIRST INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the first input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion: his chiseled chibi jawline; his stern flat closed-mouth expression with no smile and no snarl; his thick dark-brown eyebrows with a slight stern furrow; his large warm steel-blue eyes with a single bright catchlight in each iris and visible whites of the eyes; his prominent slightly-hooked nose; his warm peachy-tan skin tone with a soft rosy blush on the cheeks above the beard line; his FULL SHORT-TRIMMED DARK-BROWN BEARD covering the chin and lower cheeks reaching the upper neck — trimmed short on the cheeks, NOT bushy, NOT wild, NOT long, NOT extended; his dark mustache continuous with the beard, covering the upper lip but not extending past the corners of the mouth; his dark-brown short hair visible only as a thin fringe at the forehead under the front edge of the mail coif; the chunky scale-mail coif covering the entire scalp, ears, and neck down to the collarbone, with chunky overlapping rounded scale-shaped plates in cool silver-gray steel painted with soft top-down highlights and warm-brown shadows under each scale's lower edge, draping around the shoulders to form a broad cowl-like collar; the sleeveless mustard-yellow and crimson-red split LIVERY TUNIC — viewer-LEFT half of the tunic chest is solid crimson-red, viewer-RIGHT half is mustard-yellow with a vertical crimson-red center stripe running collar to hem, simple round neckline, hem reaches mid-thigh — this red+mustard split livery tunic remains visibly DOMINANT on the chest, fully visible; the existing brown leather forearm bracers wrapping wrist to mid-forearm on both arms with a single visible cross-strap binding per bracer; the existing quilted brown leather short sleeves on both upper arms (per CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION above); the existing wide warm-brown leather belt with square dark-iron buckle (per CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION above); the existing brown leather hip pouch on the anatomical-RIGHT hip with flap closure and brass stud (per CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION above); the arming sword held in the anatomical-RIGHT hand — heavy warm-brown leather-wrapped sword grip, sword blade at the striking end painted slightly the cool silver-gray steel of the blade darker than the warm-brown leather grip, simple steel cross-guard (pyramidal cool-gray steel cones) protruding radially from the sword blade with a single spike on the top apex, sword blade extending downward and toward viewer-LEFT with the blade tip near hip height (the new leather grip wrap wraps the upper third of the sword grip per Edit Goal item 2; the sword blade shape and the six steel spikes remain unchanged); the round wooden buckler-style shield held in the anatomical-LEFT arm — approximately 24 inches in diameter, warm-brown wooden plank face with faint vertical wood grain, central cool-gray steel boss as a small dome, the existing ring of eight dark-iron rim rivets per CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION (the new four iron cross strap-bands per Edit Goal item 3 are added between the boss and the rim rivets); the dark blue / muted steel-blue knee-length trousers slightly loose at the cuff with soft painted vertical fold lines; the chunky warm-brown leather boots reaching mid-calf with a darker folded ankle cuff at the top and wide flat soles. ===== POSE (must match the first input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees to the viewer's left so the character's anatomical right side faces camera, weight planted evenly on both feet with a very slight forward lean. THE SWORD ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) is extended forward and slightly DOWNWARD, elbow bent at roughly 120 degrees. The hand grips the leather wrap of the arming sword's grip just below the cross-guard; the sword blade extends downward and toward viewer-LEFT with the blade tip near hip height; the knuckles of the gripping hand are visible to camera. THE SHIELD ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) is bent at the elbow, with the forearm angled across the body bringing the round wooden shield up to upper-chest / shoulder height. The shield's flat face presents toward the viewer at a slight three-quarter angle, NOT perfectly flat to camera, NOT edge-on. The hand and forearm are concealed BEHIND the shield. The pose reads as a calm guard stance: sword ready but not raised, shield up but not braced for impact. The pose is NOT a swing, NOT a charge, NOT a block in motion — both arms are static and balanced. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline around the entire character in deep warm brown-black with even weight, slight thickening in shadowed recesses, slight thinning where forms catch the key light. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match the SECOND input image exactly — the second input is the L1_Base scale anchor) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Character occupies approximately 66.8% of canvas vertical height (~684px tall) and approximately 60.8% of canvas horizontal width (~623px wide, including the sword and shield extending the silhouette envelope). TOP of mail coif at approximately 16.6% from top of canvas (~170px headroom). BOTTOM of boots at approximately 16.6% from bottom of canvas (~170px below). Character vertically centered, NOT bottom-anchored. Approximately 18.3% empty margin viewer-LEFT, approximately 20.9% empty margin viewer-RIGHT. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop the head or feet, DO NOT shift to bottom edge. Clean white background with no environmental context, no ground plane, no scenery, minimal contact shadow, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT revert any L2 feature: DO NOT remove the quilted leather short sleeves and re-expose bare biceps; DO NOT replace the leather belt with a rope cord; DO NOT remove the hip pouch on the anatomical-RIGHT hip; DO NOT remove the eight dark-iron rim rivets on the shield; DO NOT remove or shrink or cover the mustard-yellow and crimson-red split livery tunic — the red+mustard chest livery must remain visibly DOMINANT on the chest; DO NOT replace the tunic with a body coat, gambeson, surcoat, or armor; DO NOT cover the tunic chest with scale mail, chain mail, or any metal armor at this level; DO NOT add scale mail, chain mail, breastplate, cuirass, or any body metal armor — the only metal additions at L3 are the single steel pauldron cap and the four iron shield strap-bands; DO NOT add a helm, steel cap, kettle helm, sallet, or any structural addition to the head — the head covering remains exactly the existing mail coif; DO NOT add a second pauldron on the anatomical-LEFT shoulder (shield-arm side) — exactly ONE pauldron at L3, on the sword-arm side only; DO NOT make the pauldron a layered multi-disc shape — it is a single rounded steel cap at L3 (layered pauldrons arrive at L4); DO NOT add scale-mail short sleeves under the pauldron — those arrive at L4; DO NOT add steel pauldron straps as fully visible chest harness — the supporting leather strap is mostly hidden under the tunic; DO NOT add deepened central blade fuller to the sword blade — those arrive at L4; DO NOT replace the sword blade with a flanged steel head — that arrives at L7; DO NOT change the cross-guard shape on the sword blade; DO NOT add a continuous iron rim band around the shield perimeter — that arrives at L4 (only the L2 ring of eight rivets and the new L3 four iron cross strap-bands at L3); DO NOT add embossed boss, sun-burst pattern, or steel-faced shield — those arrive at L7 / L5; DO NOT replace the wooden shield face with metal; DO NOT change the round shield to a heater shield, kite shield, tower shield, square shield, or any non-round shape; DO NOT add painted heraldry, emblems, or coats of arms to the shield face; DO NOT add a second hip pouch — exactly ONE pouch on the anatomical-RIGHT hip; DO NOT add a second knife sheath — exactly ONE sheath on the anatomical-LEFT hip; DO NOT add the knife sheath on the same side as the existing pouch; DO NOT make the knife handle gold, jeweled, or ornate — plain bone-handle only; DO NOT add a leather X-strap chest harness, baldric, sword belt, or any additional chest strap (the only chest crossing element is the thin pauldron-supporting leather strap mostly hidden under the tunic); DO NOT add any bevor / steel collar / steel throat plate — those arrive at L5; DO NOT add steel vambraces or replace the leather bracers — those arrive at L5; DO NOT add steel sabatons, greaves, leg armor, or any leg metal at this level; DO NOT add gold trim, gold buckle, gold accents, gold rivets, gold band, or any gold anywhere; DO NOT add gemstones, jewels, embroidery, runes, engraved patterns, or filigree; DO NOT change any facial feature including the jawline, brow shape, eye color, eye shape, nose shape, mouth shape, mustache shape, beard shape, beard length, or skin tone; DO NOT grow the beard into a wild bushy beard or extend it past the upper neck; DO NOT remove the beard or trim it to clean-shaven; DO NOT change the hair color or hair fringe under the coif; DO NOT change the livery tunic colors, panel layout, neckline, or hem; DO NOT change the trouser color (dark blue / muted steel-blue) or boot color (warm brown); DO NOT swap the hand holding the sword; DO NOT swap the hand holding the shield; DO NOT change the chibi proportions or head-to-body ratio; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure; DO NOT change the background, add scenery, add a ground plane, or add cast shadows; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, or non-white — the background must be clean opaque white; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
